feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Suite
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Suite Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Set Up Integration Suite Error APIMGT100: Activation of API Management capability has failed.
entry.content:
When I activate the capabilities in Integration Suite, I encounter the following error: "Error APIMGT100: Activation of API Management capability has failed. Please try again later."

Does anyone have a solution for this issue?
Thank you in advance!

SAP Integration Suite SAP Integration Strategy

entry.title: How to manually set message tatus in CPI MessageProcessingLog?
entry.content:
Hi,

I want to send back to my requester custom reply if my integration logic works correctly. Requester connecting to Inflow via HTTP adapter, as HTTP is synchronous, when message ends I want to send custom reply for example:
<reply>

<Message> All good</Message>

<StatusCode>200</StatusCode>

</reply>

I found that it's possible to do only when message ends with COMPLETED status.

If I have exception subprocess where I also adjusting response as example :

<reply>

<Message> Error</Message>

<StatusCode>404</StatusCode>

</reply>

, it should end with End Message. Otherwise using End Error it will send Default Error message to requester (as synchronous HTTP response).

Logic works but in that case all messages neither they are failed or completed are COMPLETED in message monitoring and that makes support monitoring very hard.

entry.title: Tutorial Design and Deploy Your First Integration Flow Error Connect request reply to receiver
entry.content:
At link tutorial: Design and Deploy Your First Integration Flow | SAP Tutorials
When I followed Step 6.4, in the Address field, I entered: https://refapps-espm-ui-cf.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/espm-cloud-web/espm.svc

However, I encountered the error shown in the image below.

To resolve this issue, I suggest using the following URL instead: https://refapp-espm-ui-cf.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/espm-cloud-web/espm.svc/

SAP Integration Suite

entry.title: Java.net.SocketTimeoutException: Read timed out While connecting to outlook account
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are trying to read email from an outlook account using mail adapter in SAP CPI/CI. While deploying it is giving error as: 

[CONTENT][CONTENT_DEPLOY][InstanceError] : {"message":"POLL_FAILED","parameters":["imaps://outlook.office365.com:993?binding=%23binding_MessageFlow_635&connectionTimeout=3000&delete=false&disconnect=true&fetchSize=20&folderName=Inbox&handleFailedMessage=true&mail.imap.auth.digest-md5.disable=true&mail.imap.auth.ntlm.disable=true&mail.imap.auth.xoauth2.disable=true&mail.imap.partialfetch=false&mail.imaps.partialfetch=false&maxMessagesPerPoll=20&mimeDecodeHeaders=true&peek=true&pollStrategy=%23mailPollStrategy_MessageFlow_635&scheduler=MessageFlow_635.pollConsumerScheduler&scheduler.cron=0+0%2F1+0-23+%3F+*+*+*&scheduler.timeZone=Etc%2FGMT&unseen=true"],"childMessageInstances":[{"message":"EXCEPTION","parameters":["javax.mail.MessagingException: * BYE JavaMail Exception: java.net.SocketTimeoutException: Read timed out (local port 51856 to address 10.158.235.175 (551ab33c-9aa4-45b0-69c2-227d), remote port 993 to address 52.97.135.98)"],"childMessageInstances":[{"message":"CAUSE","parameters":["com.sun.mail.iap.ConnectionException: * BYE JavaMail Exception: java.net.SocketTimeoutException: Read timed out (local port 51856 to address 10.158.235.175 (551ab33c-9aa4-45b0-69c2-227d), remote port 993 to address 52.97.135.98)"]}]}]}

Please help to analyze this error.

 

Thanks & Regards,

Mayank

entry.title: SAP CPI JDBC Connection to Azure Synapse DB failure - "The driver received an unexpected pre-login"
entry.content:
Good day,

I am looking for some assistance regarding JDBC connection to Azure Synapse DB from SAP CPI.

I have setup JDBC connection as follows via Cloud Connector.

Cloud Connector:

JDBC on SAP CPI:

SAP CPI:

When I run my integration flow I get the following error:

org.springframework.jdbc.CannotGetJdbcConnectionException: Failed to obtain JDBC Connection; nested exception is com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerException: The TCP/IP connection to the host localhost, port 1111 has failed. Error: "The driver received an unexpected pre-login response. Verify the connection properties and check that an instance of SQL Server is running on the host and accepting TCP/IP connections at the port. This driver can be used only with SQL Server 2005 or later.". ClientConnectionId:8344ef29-faec-4131-b3b0-0919c1c3246e, cause: com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerException: The TCP/IP connection to the host localhost, port 1111 has failed. Error: "The driver received an unexpected pre-login response. Verify the connection properties and check that an instance of SQL Server is running on the host and accepting TCP/IP connections at the port. This driver can be used only with SQL Server 2005 or later.". ClientConnectionId:8344ef29-faec-4131-b3b0-0919c1c3246e

Has anyone experienced this before and if so how do I resolve?

entry.title: Exporting contents creates an Hash
entry.content:
Hi 
When exporting a Package from SAP Integration Suite. It creates an Hash file containing to Hash's

How are these Hashes computed - from what content ?

An example is attached.

 The hash looks like a SHA-256 but from what is it created ?

entry.title: I Want to Switch to IT as SAP MM Consultant from an End User
entry.content:
Hi

I Worked as Section In-charge around 09 Years in Stores and i also worked in Migration period as End user for UAT confirmations and Know the Scenarios of P2P now i want to migrate to IT as SAP MM Consultant and I need real time project guidance.  

Kindly Guide me.

entry.title: How to host SAP PI/PO as a web service ?
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

I am working on a scenario involving integration between an SAP system and a third-party system, where SAP PI/PO is used as the middleware. My objective is to provide the third-party sender with an API of SAP PI/PO, which they will use to send data. The data received by PI/PO will be passed on to an OData service developed by an ABAPer.

Requirements:

  • The third-party sender will call the PI/PO API to send data.
  • PI/PO will need to send this data to the OData service.
  • The OData service requires a CSRF token in the request headers when receiving data.

Since I am new to SAP PI/PO, I would appreciate some guidance on the following:

  1. API Creation in PI/PO:

    • How can I create and expose the API in SAP PI/PO for the third-party system to send data?
    • What are the steps to configure the communication channel and integration flow?

  2. Routing Data to OData Service:

    • What is the best way to route the data from PI/PO to the OData service on the SAP backend?
    • Are there any specific configurations for the receiver communication channel to handle OData service calls?

  3. CSRF Token Handling:

    • The OData service requires a CSRF token to be included in the request headers.
    • How can I fetch the CSRF token from the OData service dynamically within PI/PO?
    • What is the recommended way to assign this token to the HTTP headers in PI/PO before sending the data to the OData service?

If anyone has experience with this type of integration, please share any best practices, detailed steps, or resources that can help me successfully implement this.


Best Regards, 

Prathmesh Sutar 
prathmeshsutar.sapcpi@gmail.com

entry.title: CPI expand query error
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to set up an API using CPI and testing it via Postman. Everything is working well except for sending a query with an expand parameter. In this case I am receiving the error "Could not find property with name: 'xxxx'". 
The query is successful in SAP GW Client. The navigation property does exist in the SAP metadata file but not in the auto-generated CPI edmx file.

Any ideas what's wrong?

Best Regards

entry.title: Sender SFTP Poll Failed
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have an issue with the Sender SFTP Poll Failed, Timeout issue after the end to end processed.
 Sender SFTP --> XI Receiver
The file is getting processed  successfully to receiver proxy but the iflow remains in error state after deployment.

entry.title: SAP CPI Connectivity issue OAuth 2.0 with Salesforce REST API using a Rojo Adapter
entry.content:
We're trying to connect to Salesforce from SAP CPI using salesforce sender adapter in QA environment, but we're getting the below error from Salesforce and iflow is not getting deployed. All the IP whitelisting and everything is done from Salesforce side. We have tried doing a workaround by maintaining the Salesforce dev credentials in SAP CPI QA environment and iflow got deployed but using the Salesforce QA credentials we're not able to deploy the iflow. Please suggest can anything be done from SAP CPI side to rectify/fix the issue. We're using BasicAuthentication and Oauth Credential name and Secure token Alias for authentication.


[CONTENT][CONTENT_DEPLOY][RuntimeError] : {"message":"EXCEPTION","parameters":["org.apache.camel.CamelException: org.apache.camel.CamelException: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no"?> <array> <error>invalid_grant</error> <error_description>authentication failure</error_description> </array> "],"childMessageInstances":[{"message":"CAUSE","parameters":["org.apache.camel.CamelException: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no"?> <array> <error>invalid_grant</error> <error_description>authentication failure</error_description> </array> "]}]}

entry.title: How to Access SAP Cloud ALM Demo Server via Trial Environment?
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to access the SAP Cloud ALM demo server, but it seems that my user ID does not currently have access to the trial server.

Could anyone please guide me on how to gain access to the SAP Cloud ALM demo server or let me know if there are any specific steps I need to follow to set it up? Any insights or assistance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your help!

Best regards,
Pooja Arjun
SAP Cloud ALMSAP Integration Strategy #SAP Integration Suite

entry.title: How to do the Encoding Conversion by using Groovy?
entry.content:
Hello  Experts,

I am now trying to implement a script in SAP Integration Suite, Cloud Integration. (Used to be SAP CPI)

The scenario I am trying to realize is the encoding conversion between Shift_JIS and UTF-8. 

I am thinking that users can just specify "Convert From" and "Convert To", and the script will do the conversion.

 

Here's the code that I wrote. However, when I did the experiment, for some reason my original file was deleted and there's no new file generate.

Could someone kindly help with how I can write the script?

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets
import java.nio.charset.Charset

def Message processData(Message message) {
    // User-editable variables
    def convertFrom = "UTF-8"  // Set this to either "UTF-8" or "Shift_JIS"
    def convertTo = "Shift_JIS"  // Set this to either "UTF-8" or "Shift_JIS"

    println("Starting charset conversion from ${convertFrom} to ${convertTo}")

    // Validate and normalize charset names
    convertFrom = normalizeCharsetName(convertFrom)
    convertTo = normalizeCharsetName(convertTo)
    
    // Get the body as a byte array to preserve the original encoding
    def bodyBytes = message.getBody(byte[])
    println("Original body size: ${bodyBytes.length} bytes")
    
    // Perform the conversion
    def convertedBytes = convertCharset(bodyBytes, convertFrom, convertTo)
    println("Converted body size: ${convertedBytes.length} bytes")
    
    // Set the converted byte array as the new body
    message.setBody(convertedBytes)
    
    // Set the output charset
    message.setProperty("CamelCharsetName", convertTo)
    
    println("Conversion complete. Headers: ${message.getHeaders()}")
    
    return message
}

def String normalizeCharsetName(String charset) {
    switch (charset.toLowerCase()) {
        case "utf-8": return "UTF-8"
        case "utf8": return "UTF-8"
        case "shift-jis": return "Shift_JIS"
        case "shift_jis": return "Shift_JIS"
        case "shiftjis": return "Shift_JIS"
        case "sjis": return "Shift_JIS"
        default: 
            println("ERROR: Unsupported charset: ${charset}")
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Unsupported charset: " + charset)
    }
}

def byte[] convertCharset(byte[] input, String fromCharset, String toCharset) {
    if (fromCharset == toCharset) {
        println("No conversion needed, charsets are the same")
        return input
    }
    
    try {
        Charset sourceCharset = Charset.forName(fromCharset)
        Charset targetCharset = Charset.forName(toCharset)
        
        String decodedString = new String(input, sourceCharset)
        byte[] result = decodedString.getBytes(targetCharset)
        
        println("Conversion successful: ${fromCharset} to ${toCharset}")
        return result
    } catch (Exception e) {
        println("ERROR: Error during conversion: ${e.message}")
        throw new RuntimeException("Error during charset conversion", e)
    }
}

 

Thank you.

entry.title: SAP Signavio integration using Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Could you please let us know if there are any standard adapters available to integrate SAP Signavio using Cloud Integration(Integration Suite).. or what all is needed from SAP Signavio side like API, or are there any standard APIs available to integrate SAP Signavio ingestions? Thank you!

Regards

Ramu

entry.title: Service broker failed to delete service binding for instance iflow-key
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am trying to subscribe to delete a service key of Process Integration Runtime in my trial environment. I am getting the below error:

Service broker failed to delete service binding for instance <key-name>: Service broker error: Service broker it-broker-rt failed with: Internal Server Error
Does anybody have any idea on this?

entry.title: S4HANA Sales Order API(A2X) taking longer processing time with huge number of line item
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have Integration between BTP-IS & S4 where we are creating Sales Orders in S4 using the API_SALES_ORDER_SRV API .

S4HANA Sales Order API (A2X) is taking longer processing time to create Sales Order with huge number of line items.

We have done lot of customizations to the api at abap end due to client requirements.

How can we fix this problem as it is taking 2 mins to create order with 100 line items.

 

SAP Integration Suite SAP BTP, ABAP environment SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP Environment 

test automation tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 

SAP S/4HANA API Management API

entry.title: Structure Formatting for Receiver in SAP CPI through Groovy
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

So, there was a requirement where the XML Structure was coming with <div></div> in one of the fields and we need to remove that, it cannot be handled by XSLT as @ symbol was expected in email id so with the help of the groovy mentioned in the blog below it was resolved.

Source Structure -

<?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8'?>
<Root>
<Root1>
<ADONumber>12345</ADONumber>
<State></State>
<AssignedTo>SRIVASTAVA Nidhi (External) <nidhi.srivastava.ext@XXXXX.eu> </AssignedTo>
<Priority></Priority>
<AdditionalComments>
<div>Comment 5</div>
</AdditionalComments>
</Root1>
</Root>

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/groovy-or-xslt-to-remove-xml-tag-not-values-in-sap-cpi-payload/qaq-p/13873348

1) Now, the issue is when there is a copy paste in the system sending the data instead of <div> <span> is coming.  Something like mentioned below, this is resolved by using XSLT before the groovy mentioned in the blog solution and it is working fine - but email id issue persisting.

<AdditionalComments><span style="color:rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.9);font-size:15px;display:inline !important;">Testing Updates after removing SPAN to DIV</span></AdditionalComments>

2) Also, when there is a mix of manual writing and copy paste in the system the field is coming like 

<AdditionalComments><div>ABC  <span style="color:rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.9);font-size:15px;display:inline !important;">Testing Updates after removing SPAN to DIV XYZ <span style="display:inline !important;">Testing Updates after removing SPAN to DIV</span></span></div></AdditionalComments>

For manual written words <div> tag is coming and for copy pasted words <span> tag is coming.

Not sure if I am able to explain my issue properly, require your expert advice.

Thanks,

Nidhi Srivastava

entry.title: SMB Adapter for on-premise file share
entry.content:
On the newly released SMB Adapter for SAP Cloud Integration (CF)...

Can this adapter connect to an on-premise SMB endpoint via SAP Cloud Connector?  That would seem the most common use case.

There doesn't seem to be a proxy type field to select "internet" or "On Premise" via SAP Cloud Connector.

entry.title: How to use FILTER condition in HTTP Receiver Adapter for SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hello Techies, 

 

I would like to bring up here one interesting Topic in this blog, Which I have faced few issues while using the HTTP Receiver Adapter in SAP CPI.

I have faced issues with the OData Adapter that GET was not successful due to some Conversion of date with Exception. So decided to use HTTP Adapter instead of OData.

By using OData adapter its pretty simple that we can filter condition while configuring the fields in OData fields selection level and based on the selected fields and filters query will be generated automatically.

But Incase of HTTP Adapter we need to manually form the Query in Param1=Value1&Param2=Value2 like this. This format will work if I want to use only with field level Query.

I want to use Filter in the Query tab I have used below format
$filter=Material='1234' 

$filter=Materialeq'1234'  but no luck 😞

Finally after so many trail and error methods below format is working and its able to fetch only particular Materials.

$filter=Material%20eq%20'1234'

The formation of the query should be separated with %20 before and after eq

So, by using the above format we can the Filter condition in HTTP Receiver condition and also SKIP and TOP also will be accepted by HTTP Adapter Query level.

$filter=Material%20eq%20'1234'&$top=10&$skip=5

 

Thanks & Regards, 

SaiBandaru

SAP Integration Suite Cloud Integration

SAP S/4HANA SAP Integration Suite Cloud Integration

entry.title: Encoding Conversion Groovy Script
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am now writing Groovy script for Encoding Conversion.

To be specific, a script for the conversion between Shift_JIS and UTF-8. 

I have completed the scripts that can do the one-way conversion from Shift_JIS to UTF-8, as well as the one-way conversion from UTF-8 to Shift_JIS.

However, when I want to write a two-way conversion script, it did not work well on Cloud Integration.

Could anyone kindly help me with this?

 

 

As your reference, here are the scripts that I wrote.

1)one-way conversion from Shift_JIS to UTF-8 (Worked Well)

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets

def Message processData(Message message) {
    // Get the body as a byte array to preserve the original encoding
    def bodyBytes = message.getBody(byte[])
    
    // Convert the Shift_JIS byte array to a UTF-8 string
    def utf8String = new String(bodyBytes, "Shift_JIS").getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8)
    
    // Set the converted UTF-8 byte array as the new body
    message.setBody(utf8String)
    
    // Set the output charset to UTF-8
    message.setProperty("CamelCharsetName", "UTF-8")
    
    return message
}

2)one-way conversion from UTF-8 to Shift_JIS (Worked Well)

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets

def Message processData(Message message) {
    // Get the body as a byte array (assuming it's in UTF-8)
    def bodyBytes = message.getBody(byte[])
    
    // Convert the UTF-8 byte array to a Shift_JIS string
    def shiftJisString = new String(bodyBytes, StandardCharsets.UTF_8).getBytes("Shift_JIS")
    
    // Set the converted Shift_JIS byte array as the new body
    message.setBody(shiftJisString)
    
    // Set the output charset to Shift_JIS
    message.setProperty("CamelCharsetName", "Shift_JIS")
    
    return message
}

 

3)two-way conversion between UTF-8 and Shift_JIS (Not Working Well)

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets

def Message processData(Message message) {
    // Define the source and target encodings here
    def sourceEncoding = "Shift_JIS"  // Change this to your source encoding
    def targetEncoding = "UTF-8"  // Change this to your target encoding
    
    // Get the body as a byte array
    def bodyBytes = message.getBody(byte[])
    
    def convertedBytes
    
    // Perform the conversion based on the specified encodings
    if (sourceEncoding == "Shift_JIS" && targetEncoding == "UTF-8") {
        convertedBytes = new String(bodyBytes, "Shift_JIS").getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8)
    } else if (sourceEncoding == "UTF-8" && targetEncoding == "Shift_JIS") {
        convertedBytes = new String(bodyBytes, StandardCharsets.UTF_8).getBytes("Shift_JIS")
    } else {
        // For other encoding combinations, use a more general approach
        convertedBytes = new String(bodyBytes, sourceEncoding).getBytes(targetEncoding)
    }
    
    // Set the converted byte array as the new body
    message.setBody(convertedBytes)
    
    // Set the output charset
    message.setProperty("CamelCharsetName", targetEncoding)
    
    return message
}

 

To add on, (3)two-way conversion between UTF-8 and Shift_JIS works well when it is SJIS to UTF-8.

However, it does not work when it is UTF-8 to SJIS.

 

Thank you in advance.
